calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-14-2023 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-14-2022 Continued To Mar-08-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Kaleb L. Judy; Declaration Of Laura Bennett (Motions)
Off Calendar Nov-04-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. MARGAUX GUNDZIK, Esq. of Lawyers for Justice, PC (Tel: 818-265-1020) for plaintiff;
Atty. KALEB L. JUDY, Esq. of Belden Blaine Raytis, LLP (Tel: 661-864-7826) for defendants.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS CENTRE FOR NEURO SKILLS, CFN SERVICES INC., and CFN SERVICES, INC.- CALIFORNIA's Motion To Transfer Venue.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' notice of motion and motion to transfer venue to Kern County is granted. Plaintiff shall pay the transfer fees.

A corporation or association may be sued in the county where the contract is made or is to be performed, or where the obligation or liability arises, or the breach occurs; or in the county where the principal place of business of such corporation is situated, subject to the power of the court to change the place of trial as in other cases. (CCP 395.5) "As the party attempting to change venue, [Defendant] bears the burden of establishing that [San Francisco] County is not a proper venue under any applicable statute. (Crestwood Behavioral Health, Inc. v. Superior Court of Alameda County (2021) 60 Cal.App.5th 1069, 1075.)

Plaintiff argues that venue is proper in San Francisco County and should not be transferred to Kern County because, "allegedly aggrieved employees for Defendants did, in fact, perform work in San Francisco County." (MPA 5:2-3.) However, Defendants argue that under sec. 395.5, venue is proper in Kern County because their principal place of business is there (Def. Exs. A-C) and because their alleged liability arose there (Bennett Decl., 9:9-13). Plaintiff also argues that under Crestwood, venue is proper in any county where an aggrieved employee worked (Opp., 5:1-2) and alleges that Defendants' employees worked in San Francisco County. However, Defendants have met their burden to show that San Francisco County is not a proper venue and provided a declaration from their Human Resources Manager which states that "None of CNS's employees work in San Francisco County. None of CNS's affiliated sister or parent companies have had locations or employees working in San Francisco County at any time in the last three years." (Bennett Decl., 8:27-28, 9:1.)

Order signed.

Judge: Vedica Puri; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/VP)
